skill.name: infsh-cli
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation.
skill.summary: Access 250+ AI models and apps through the belt CLI for image, video, LLM, and search tasks.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill consists entirely of Markdown documentation with example CLI commands for the inference.sh platform. The 'shell execution' and 'pipe to shell' findings are standard CLI installation patterns documented with manual install alternatives. No executable code exists in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Pipe-to-shell installation pattern documented
low_findings.description: The skill documents `curl ... | sh` for installing the belt CLI. While this is a standard pattern for CLI tool distribution, users should review install scripts. The skill also provides a manual install alternative with SHA-256 checksum verification and optional Sigstore signature verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation examples for installing a legitimate commercial CLI tool. The pattern is standard for open-source CLI distribution and the skill explicitly documents the installer's behavior.

low_findings.title: API key environment variable usage
low_findings.description: The skill documents using INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD authentication. This is standard practice but users should ensure secrets are managed securely.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable usage for API keys is the documented authentication method for the platform. This is expected behavior for a CLI tool, not a security vulnerability.

content.user_title: Run AI Apps from Your Terminal
content.value_statement: Accessing multiple AI models requires juggling different APIs and tools. This skill provides a unified CLI to run 250+ AI apps for image, video, text, and search tasks.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using models like FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Stable Diffusion
Create videos with Veo, Seedance, Wan, and OmniHuman
Call LLMs including Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Kimi via OpenRouter
Perform web search using Tavily and Exa
Upload local files for processing by AI apps
limitations: Requires a free inference.sh account and API key
Usage is subject to credit-based pricing on the platform
Local file uploads are limited to supported file types per app
Some apps may have rate limits or processing time constraints

use_cases.title: Content Creator
use_cases.description: Generate images and videos for social media or marketing without managing GPU infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Developer
use_cases.description: Integrate AI capabilities into scripts and CI/CD pipelines using simple CLI commands.

use_cases.title: Researcher
use_cases.description: Access multiple LLMs and search APIs from one tool for comparison and analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the belt CLI to generate an image with FLUX. The prompt should be: a futuristic city at sunset with flying cars.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video using Veo 3.1 with the prompt: aerial drone shot over a tropical beach at golden hour.

prompt_templates.title: Query an LLM
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask Claude via OpenRouter to explain the difference between machine learning and deep learning in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily to search for the latest news about AI regulation in 2026 and return the top 5 results.

output_examples.input: belt app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut"}'
output_examples.output: Image file saved with URL containing the generated image of a cat in a space suit floating among stars.

output_examples.input: belt app run tavily/search-assistant --input '{"query": "latest AI news"}'
output_examples.output: List of search results with titles, snippets, and source URLs for the top 10 articles about recent AI developments.

output_examples.input: belt app run openrouter/claude-sonnet-45 --input '{"prompt": "Explain quantum computing"}'
output_examples.output: A detailed explanation of quantum computing covering qubits, superposition, and entanglement in accessible language.

best_practices: Set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable in CI/CD environments instead of passing keys directly
Use version pinning (e.g., app@1.0.0) to ensure reproducible results across runs
Generate sample input files with belt app sample to understand the expected schema before running
anti_patterns: Avoid hardcoding API keys in scripts - use environment variables or config files
Do not run apps without checking the input schema first with belt app get
Avoid running long tasks synchronously - use --no-wait and check status with belt task get

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: The belt CLI is the command-line interface for inference.sh, a platform that provides access to 250+ AI models and apps through a single tool.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to run AI apps?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens in the cloud on inference.sh infrastructure. You only need a terminal and an internet connection.

faq.question: How do I authenticate?
faq.answer: Run belt login to authenticate via browser, or set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for automated environments.

faq.question: Is the install script safe?
faq.answer: The installer downloads from cli.inference.sh, verifies SHA-256 checksums, and optionally verifies Sigstore signatures. You can also install manually by downloading the binary directly.

faq.question: Can I use local files as input?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI automatically uploads local files when you provide a file path instead of a URL in the input JSON.

faq.question: How do I check task status for long-running apps?
faq.answer: Use belt task get <task-id> to check status, or run apps with --no-wait to submit and check later.
